creator: rseguin@rsv.ricoh.com
text:
There appears to be an intermittent bug with the dmesg buffer or dmesg program.
About 1 time in 15, the output from dmesg is corrupted with binary characters
which make the output unsuitable for parsing to obtain information about the machine.
The output is still recognizable as the normal kernel message output, but
it is littered with garbage binary characters.


bash-2.03# /sbin/dmesg
\^D +\^D\^D\^A\^P\^D\^D\^H\^D\^B\^H  @\M^@\^P\^F\^H\M-`\^O\M-p\^_\^H\M-p\M^?\^O\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^F\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^E\^L\^D\^A\^P@ \^D\^P\^H\^P \^B@\M^@@\M^@\M^@\^P@\^A@\^B\M^@ \^P\^P\^A\M^@\^AFACS@ 1986, 1989, 1991, 1993
        The Regents of the University of California. All rights reserved.

Along with this bug is the fact that the kernel messages in /var/log/messages
are incorrect.  The kernel messages indicate that there is a DVD-RAM drive,
when there is none.


Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: RTC BIOS diagnostic error 2
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: ad0: 16479MB <Maxtor 91728D8> [33483/16/63] at ata0-master using UDMA33
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: daasync: ONE LUN device to handle as TWO LUN
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: Mounting root from ufs:/dev/ad0s1a
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: da0 at ahc0 bus 0 target 0 lun 0
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: da0: <HITACHI GF-1050 S00F> Removable CD-ROM SCSI-2 device
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: da0: 10.000MB/s transfers (10.000MHz, offset 8)
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: da0: Attempt to query device size failed: NOT READY, Medium not present
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: cd0 at ahc0 bus 0 target 0 lun 0
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: cd0: <HITACHI GF-1050 S00F> Removable CD-ROM SCSI-2 device
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: cd0: 10.000MB/s transfers (10.000MHz, offset 8)
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: cd0: Attempt to query device size failed: NOT READY, Medium not present
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: fxp0: starting DAD for fe80:0001::02d0:b7ff:fe1a:8de0
Jun 26 09:30:15 FBSD40 /kernel: fxp0: DAD complete for fe80:0001::02d0:b7ff:fe1a:8de0 - no duplicates found                                                     


Notice the da0 messages?  There is NO DVD-RAM hooked up to this machine.

How-To-Repeat: 1. Reboot machine.
2. tail -100 /var/log/messages.
